etymology.description: semantic 女(女) (nǚ, "woman") + phonetic 兆(兆) (zhào) combined to indicate an elegant woman or handsome person
etymology.interpretation: The character 姚(yáo, "handsome; elegant") combines the semantic 女(女) (nǚ, "woman") indicating gender or grace with the phonetic 兆(兆) (zhào), which does not directly inform the meaning but provides the pronunciation. The resulting sense of "handsome" or "elegant" suggests qualities of beauty or stylishness traditionally associated with women or persons of elegance.

examples.chinese: 他的举止很姚(他的舉止很姚)。
姚姓在中国很常见(姚姓在中國很常見)。
examples.pinyin: Yáo xìng zài Zhōngguó hěn chángjiàn.
examples.english: The surname Yao is very common in China.

examples.chinese: 他是一位姚士(他是一位姚士)，总是吸引很多目光(總是吸引很多目光)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì yī wèi yáoshì, zǒng shì xīyǐn hěn duō mùguāng.
examples.english: He is a handsome gentleman who always attracts a lot of attention.
